batch_summary: In a comedic yet tense confrontation, Joey insists his father, Joey Sr., and his girlfriend, Gloria, stay at his apartment under his strict rules, forbidding them from sleeping together. Joey rearranges the sleeping arrangements, forcing himself and a sarcastic Chandler to share a hide-a-bed. Privately, Joey demands his father either end the six-year affair or confess to his mother. Later that night, Chandler is left highly uncomfortable as Joey strips down to his underwear to sleep.
cumulative_summary: Joey's father, Joey Sr., and his girlfriend, Gloria, arrive at Joey's apartment, sparking a tense but humorous family dispute. To prevent them from sleeping together, Joey insists they stay under his roof, enforcing strict rules. He rearranges the living situation: Joey Sr. takes Joey's room, Gloria stays in Chandler's room, and Joey and Chandler are relegated to the living room's pull-out sofa. Once they are alone, Joey confronts his father, firmly demanding that he either end his six-year affair with Gloria or come clean to Joey's mother. The night ends on a comedic note as Joey and Chandler share the cramped hide-a-bed, with Joey making Chandler deeply uncomfortable by stripping down to his underwear to get comfortable.

batch_summary: While sharing the hide-a-bed, Joey confides in Chandler about his fear of inheriting his father's unfaithful habits. Chandler reassures Joey of his strength and integrity, though he quickly rebuffs Joey's attempt to cuddle. The next morning, Joey Sr.'s mistress, Ronni, arrives at Monica's apartment to use the shower. Learning that Chandler is occupying Joey's bathroom, Rachel eagerly rushes over to Joey's apartment to use theirs, greeting Joey Sr. in the kitchen.

batch_summary: Rachel's attempt to get even with Chandler backfires when she accidentally barges in on a showering Joey instead. Meanwhile, at Central Perk, the gang reluctantly confesses to Phoebe that they dislike her highly analytical boyfriend, Roger. Back at the apartment, Joey's mother, Gloria, unexpectedly visits. Instead of being grateful, she angrily reprimands Joey for interfering in his father's affair, revealing she has known about the infidelity all along and preferred the status quo.

Hoping to catch Chandler off-guard in the bathroom as payback, Rachel barges in, only to accidentally surprise a showering Joey instead. Meanwhile, at Central Perk, the gang awkwardly confesses to Phoebe that they strongly dislike her overly analytical boyfriend, Roger. batch_summary: Gloria explains to a bewildered Joey that his father's affair has actually made him happier and more attentive at home. Meanwhile, Ronni also expresses her desire to return to the previous status quo, explaining that Joey Sr.'s guilt made him a more loving partner. Realizing his well-intentioned interference only complicated things, Joey apologizes. The tense situation softens as Ronni comforts Joey, and he sweetly reassures her of her beauty compared to his mother, restoring a bizarre sense of harmony.

batch_summary: At Central Perk, Phoebe reluctantly confesses to Roger that her friends find him "intense and creepy." Initially calm, Roger quickly turns defensive and launches into a harsh, condescending tirade. Meanwhile, back at the apartment, the gang asks Joey about his conversation with his father.
